text.title: agents-react-pattern Core Rules
text.summary: Migrated testable rules for agents-react-pattern. Verify and refine before relying on for production.

rule.statement: Every ReAct agent MUST set a hard iteration limit (20-50 steps) and a per-run cost cap before entering the loop. The agent MUST raise an exception when either limit is hit rather than returning a partial result silently.
rule.rationale: Without iteration limits, agents get stuck retrying failing tool calls indefinitely. Without cost caps, a single unbounded loop can burn the entire API budget in minutes on expensive tool calls (image gen, video gen, external APIs).

rule.statement: Tool descriptions MUST be written as if for a person unfamiliar with the codebase: include the exact parameter names, types, and what the function returns. Tool MUST return explicit error text (not raise exceptions) — agents handle text errors far better than stack traces.
rule.rationale: Tool argument hallucination is the most common ReAct failure mode. Clear descriptions reduce hallucination. Text error returns allow the agent to understand what went wrong and try alternative arguments rather than crash.
